title: MUA: Mobile Ultra-detailed Animatable Avatars
problem: Building photorealistic, animatable full-body digital humans that simultaneously achieve ultra-high fidelity and low computational cost for resource-constrained platforms.
key_idea: A novel animatable avatar representation called Wavelet-guided Multi-level Spatial Factorized Blendshapes, coupled with a distillation pipeline that transfers motion-aware clothing dynamics and fine-grained appearance details from a high-quality teacher model to a compact, efficient representation.
method: Wavelet-guided Multi-level Spatial Factorized Blendshapes representation and a corresponding distillation pipeline; combines multi-level wavelet spectral decomposition with low-rank structural factorization in texture space.
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
results_summary: Achieves up to 2000X lower computational cost and a 10X smaller model size than the original high-quality teacher avatar model; achieves over 180 FPS on a desktop PC and real-time native on-device performance at 24 FPS on a standalone Meta Quest 3.

title: A multimodal and temporal foundation model for virtual patient representations at healthcare system scale
problem: No existing model integrates the full breadth and temporal depth of the clinical record into a unified patient representation.
key_idea: Apollo is a multimodal temporal foundation model that learns a unified representation space integrating over 100 thousand unique medical events, images, and clinical text to generate virtual patient representations capturing entire care journeys.
method: Training and evaluation of a multimodal temporal foundation model (Apollo) on longitudinal hospital records, using feature attribution techniques for interpretability and semantic similarity search for retrieval tasks.
dataset_or_scenario: Over three decades of longitudinal hospital records from a major US hospital system, composed of 25 billion records from 7.2 million patients, representing 28 distinct medical modalities and 12 major medical specialties.
results_summary: Evaluated on 322 prognosis and retrieval tasks from a held-out test set of 1.4 million patients; demonstrated forecasting for disease onset (95 tasks), progression (78 tasks), treatment response (59 tasks), adverse events (17 tasks), and hospital operations (12 tasks); evaluated on 61 retrieval tasks.

title: Latent Phase-Shift Rollback: Inference-Time Error Correction via Residual Stream Monitoring and KV-Cache Steering
problem: Large language models frequently commit unrecoverable reasoning errors mid-generation, where subsequent tokens compound rather than correct the mistake.
key_idea: LPSR detects abrupt directional reversals (phase shifts) in the residual stream at a critical layer and responds by rolling back the KV-cache and injecting a pre-computed steering vector.
method: At each generation step, monitor the residual stream at a critical layer lcrit, detect phase shifts using a cosine-similarity + entropy dual gate, and rollback the KV-cache while injecting a pre-computed steering vector; no fine-tuning, gradients, or additional forward passes required.
dataset_or_scenario: MATH-500
results_summary: LPSR achieves 44.0% on MATH-500 with an 8B model versus 28.8% for standard AR (+15.2 pp; McNemar χ² = 66.96, p < 10⁻¹⁵); outperforms prompted self-correction (19.8%) by +24.2 pp (χ² = 89.4, p ≈ 0); outperforms Best-of-16 by +7.8 pp at 5.4× lower token cost; surpasses standard 70B model (35.2%) with 8.75× fewer parameters at ~3× token budget; detection AUC peaks at layer 14 (0.718), task accuracy peaks at layer 16 (44.0% vs. 29.2%).

title: Non-linear Lie Conformal Algebras and One-Loop Corrections of self-dual Yang-Mills amplitudes
problem: Understanding QCD collinear singularities in the context of celestial holography and their interpretation via operator product expansions in a two-dimensional CFT
key_idea: We reformulate the algebraic structures arising from QCD collinear singularities using the formalism of non-linear Lie conformal algebras.
method: Reformulation using the formalism of non-linear Lie conformal algebras
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
results_summary: Not specified
best_fit_category: theoretical physics / mathematical physics / celestial holography

title: Advancing Vision Transformer with Enhanced Spatial Priors
problem: Self-Attention in Vision Transformers lacks explicit spatial priors and suffers from quadratic computational complexity, limiting applicability.
key_idea: EVT enhances spatial modeling in Vision Transformers by replacing Manhattan distance decay with Euclidean distance decay and substituting decomposed attention with spatially-independent token grouping.
method: Euclidean distance decay for spatial information modeling; spatially-independent grouping for attention computation instead of horizontal and vertical decomposition.
dataset_or_scenario: Image Classification, Object Detection, Instance Segmentation, and Semantic Segmentation
results_summary: EVT achieves 86.6% top1-acc on ImageNet-1k without additional training data.

title: Wasserstein Distributionally Robust Risk-Sensitive Estimation via Conditional Value-at-Risk
problem: Risk-sensitive estimation of an unknown signal x from an observed signal y when the joint distribution is unknown but lies in a Wasserstein ambiguity set
key_idea: Proposing a distributionally robust estimation framework that minimizes the worst-case conditional value-at-risk (CVaR) of squared estimation error over a type-2 Wasserstein ball of distributions.
method: Computing affine estimators by solving a tractable semidefinite program, applicable when the nominal distribution at the center of the Wasserstein ball is finitely supported
dataset_or_scenario: Wholesale electricity price forecasting task using real market data
results_summary: The proposed estimators deliver lower out-of-sample CVaR of squared error compared to existing methods

title: Dissipative Preparation of Correlated Quantum States in Dipolar Rydberg Arrays
problem: Preparing correlated quantum states in many-body systems is essential but challenging.
key_idea: A dissipative protocol engineers nonreciprocal, energy-selective transitions using controllable auxiliary atoms to steer dipolar quantum systems toward desired many-body states.
method: Introducing two types of controllable dissipative auxiliary atoms that act as nonreciprocal excitation and de-excitation channels, enabling a directional walk in Hilbert space.
dataset_or_scenario: Neutral atoms in dipolar Rydberg arrays
results_summary: Enables stabilization of states across the many-body spectrum, not limited to the ground state and requiring no a priori knowledge of the Hamiltonian.

title: Fast and Forgettable: A Controlled Study of Novices' Performance, Learning, Workload, and Emotion in AI-Assisted and Human Pair Programming Paradigms
problem: The unforeseen consequences of AI code generation on computing students' learning, performance, workload, and emotion, particularly amid declining use of human pair programming and lack of empirical evidence on AI's educational impact.
key_idea: A controlled study comparing novices' performance, learning, workload, and emotion when pair programming with a human teammate versus with GitHub Copilot.
method: Controlled pair programming study with within-subjects design: 22 participants wrote Python code under time pressure in three conditions—human pair, AI-assisted (GitHub Copilot), and individual—and were retested after one week; subjective (workload, emotion) and objective (performance, retest performance) measures collected.
dataset_or_scenario: 22 novice participants writing Python code under time pressure in teams of two and individually with GitHub Copilot for 20 minutes each, followed by a one-week retention interval and individual retest.
results_summary: Participants performed significantly better with GitHub Copilot than with their human teammate; several dimensions of workload were significantly reduced with Copilot; emotional effect of the human teammate was significantly more positive and arousing; there was a nonsignificant absolute retest performance reduction in the AI condition and a larger retest performance decrement in the AI condition.

title: Hamiltonian dynamics from pure dissipation
problem: How internal Hamiltonian (unitary, reversible) dynamics can emerge from purely dissipative (non-unitary, irreversible) open quantum system evolution without an explicit Hamiltonian term.
key_idea: Bounded-norm dissipative generators with nontraceless jump operators and zero explicit Hamiltonian can approximate Hamiltonian dynamics within ε error in diamond norm.
method: Analysis of GKSL-form Lindbladians with zero Hamiltonian term but nontraceless jump operators; diamond norm error analysis; geometric lower bound proof for time scaling; construction-based approximation.
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
results_summary: Dissipative generators approximate Hamiltonian dynamics within ε error using O(t²/ε) evolution time; this scaling is proven necessary and optimal in the worst case from a geometric perspective.

title: AtomTwin.jl: a physics-native digital twin framework for neutral-atom quantum processors
problem: Developing accurate, scalable, and hardware-aware simulation and control frameworks for neutral-atom quantum processors
key_idea: AtomTwin.jl is a physics-native digital twin framework that models atoms, optical tweezers, laser fields, motion, interactions, and noise directly from physical geometry and parameters—without requiring manual Hamiltonian specification.
method: Open-source Julia package providing hardware-level instruction sequences, high-performance solvers for coupled quantum and classical dynamics, and a ready-to-use model for ytterbium-171 atoms in an extensible architecture.
dataset_or_scenario: Preparation of a logical Bell state in the [[4,2,2]] error-detecting code with four ^171Yb atoms in moveable tweezers
results_summary: Not specified

title: HybridGen: Efficient LLM Generative Inference via CPU-GPU Hybrid Computing
problem: KV caches for modern LLMs grow to hundreds of gigabytes, stressing memory capacity and bandwidth; existing KV cache pruning and offloading solutions underutilize hardware by relying solely on either GPU or CPU for attention computing and do not adequately leverage CPU local memory for KV cache storage.
key_idea: HybridGen enables CPU-GPU collaborative attention on systems with expanded tiered memory (e.g., CXL memory) to efficiently handle long-context LLM inference.
method: Introduces attention logit parallelism, a feedback-driven scheduler, and semantic-aware KV cache mapping to address multi-dimensional attention dependencies, CPU-GPU load imbalance, and NUMA penalty of tiered memories.
dataset_or_scenario: Three LLM models with eleven different sizes on three GPU platforms with a CXL-expanded memory
results_summary: HybridGen outperforms six state-of-the-art KV cache management methods by 1.41x--3.2x on average while maintaining superior accuracy.
best_fit_category: systems for AI / hardware-aware LLM inference

title: Fractional motions of an active particle on the quantum vortex
problem: Understanding the diffusive motion of active particles driven by quantum vortices on superfluid helium, particularly under viscoelastic memory, thermal noise, vortex forces, viscous dissipation, and confinement.
key_idea: Analytical modeling of active particle dynamics using fractional (power-law kernel) viscoelastic memory to capture anomalous diffusion in quantum vortex environments.
method: Analytical investigation involving fractional calculus-based modeling with power-law kernels, solving for joint probability density under various force configurations (vortex force, thermal noise, viscous dissipation, harmonic confinement).
dataset_or_scenario: Experimental observations of active particles driven by quantum vortices on the surface of superfluid helium.
results_summary: Analytical solutions for the joint probability density are obtained in two distinct time regimes.

title: Low-noise Pauli-consistent ensemble Monte Carlo for graphene with electron-electron scattering
problem: High computational cost of Pauli-consistent ensemble Monte Carlo simulations for graphene when including explicit intraband electron-electron scattering, limiting large-ensemble low-noise studies
key_idea: A sampled-partner approximation that replaces the full partner-cell sum with uniform sampling from the instantaneous ensemble while preserving the event-level collision step.
method: Ensemble Monte Carlo with sampled-partner approximation for electron-electron scattering; discretized momentum-space grid; comparison against full-sum reference simulation
dataset_or_scenario: Graphene with explicit intraband electron-electron scattering
metrics: noise level in ensemble-averaged time traces
results_summary: Close agreement with the full-sum reference and substantial reduction in computational cost, enabling large-ensemble low-noise simulations where systematic oscillatory components become clearly resolved.
limitations: Oscillations in ensemble-averaged time traces are numerical artifacts arising from deterministic drift on the discretized momentum-space grid.

title: Bosonization, vertex operators and maximal violation of the Bell-CHSH inequality in wedge regions
problem: Achieving maximal violation of the Bell-CHSH inequality using physically realizable operators in quantum field theory
key_idea: The vertex operators of a chiral boson in 1+1 dimensions provide an explicit realization of dichotomic, bounded, Hermitian operators that saturate the Tsirelson bound of the Bell-CHSH inequality in the vacuum state.
method: Analysis of vertex operators in chiral bosonic conformal field theory within 1+1 dimensional quantum field theory
dataset_or_scenario: Wedge regions in 1+1 dimensional spacetime; vacuum state of a chiral boson
results_summary: Vertex operators saturate the Tsirelson bound of the Bell-CHSH inequality in the vacuum state.
best_fit_category: quantum field theory foundations / quantum information in relativistic settings

title: An adaptive discretization algorithm for locally optimal experimental design with constraints
problem: Locally optimal experimental design under constraints, such as budget or performance constraints
key_idea: An iterative adaptive discretization algorithm that refines the discretization by adding an approximate violator of a sufficient ε-optimality condition.
method: Iterative adaptive discretization; solving discretized constrained-design subproblems and refining the discretization using approximate violators of an ε-optimality condition
dataset_or_scenario: Experimental design problems from chemical engineering featuring time and yield constraints
results_summary: The algorithm converges to an optimal design when ε = 0 and finitely terminates at an ε-optimal design when ε > 0; it requires less computational effort due to smaller-dimensional nonlinear subproblems solved only approximately and only in selected iterations; it covers a larger class of constraints than existing algorithms; demonstrates good convergence properties.

title: Learning the Riccati solution operator for time-varying LQR via Deep Operator Networks
problem: Repeated numerical solution of differential Riccati equations in finite-horizon Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) problems is computationally expensive, especially for parametric or real-time applications.
key_idea: Replace repeated numerical integration of the differential Riccati equation with a learned operator surrogate that maps time-dependent system parameters to the Riccati trajectory.
method: DeepONet architectures tailored for matrix-valued, time-dependent problems, combined with a progressive learning strategy for scalability with respect to system dimension.
dataset_or_scenario: Time-invariant and time-varying LQR problems
results_summary: The proposed approach achieves high accuracy and strong generalization across a wide range of system configurations, while delivering substantial computational speedups compared to classical solvers.
best_fit_category: optimal control, operator learning, data-driven control

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Maximizing Quantum Fisher Information in Time-Dependent Many-Body Systems
problem: Maximizing Quantum Fisher Information (QFI) in time-dependent many-body systems, which is hindered by non-commutativity, control complexity, and exponential Hilbert space growth.
key_idea: A physics-informed neural network framework that learns counter-diabatic quantum dynamics by combining variational PINNs with Magnus-expansion-based time-ordered evolution to infer the adiabatic gauge potential and scheduling function while enforcing Euler-Lagrange structure.
method: Variational physics-informed neural network (PINN) formulation integrated with Magnus-expansion treatment of time-ordered evolution to enforce Euler-Lagrange structure and infer adiabatic gauge potential and scheduling function.
dataset_or_scenario: Driven spin Hamiltonians including nearest-neighbor, dipolar, and trapped-ion-inspired interactions, for systems of up to six qubits.
results_summary: The framework systematically improves over reference solutions based only on the Euler-Lagrange condition, yielding high normalized QFI, favorable fidelity and extremal-balance metrics, and small physical residuals; learning the scheduling function provides a clear performance advantage in most cases, and q=3 emerges as a particularly challenging regime.
limitations: Scalability is limited by the exponential growth of the operator space and by automatic-differentiation costs.

title: Bayesian experimental design: grouped geometric pooled posterior via ensemble Kalman methods
problem: Nested inference in Bayesian experimental design for complex physical systems is computationally prohibitive when estimating expected information gain or its gradients, due to heterogeneous posteriors induced by outer samples.
key_idea: We propose a grouped geometric pooled posterior framework that partitions outer samples into groups and constructs a pooled proposal for each group, improving proposal-target alignment without increasing forward-model evaluation cost.
method: Grouped geometric pooled posterior framework with tailored ensemble Kalman inversion (EKI) and a conservative importance-sampling quality diagnostic to guide grouping.
dataset_or_scenario: Gaussian-linear and high-dimensional network-based model discrepancy calibration problems
results_summary: The method yields more accurate and stable estimators while keeping cost comparable to amortized approaches.

title: QRAFTI: An Agentic Framework for Empirical Research in Quantitative Finance
problem: Supporting empirical research in quantitative finance—specifically equity factor research on large financial panel datasets—and enabling replication, signal discovery, and standardized reporting.
key_idea: A multi-agent framework that emulates a quantitative research team by integrating a panel data research toolkit with MCP servers exposing data access, factor construction, and custom coding as callable tools.
method: Multi-agent system using chained tool calls and reflection-based planning, integrated with MCP servers for data access, factor construction, and custom coding operations.
dataset_or_scenario: Large financial panel datasets
results_summary: Not specified

title: A primality test for $Kp^\ell - 1$ numbers
problem: Deterministic primality testing for integers of the form $N = c_D k p^{\ell} - 1$
key_idea: We develop an algebraic framework over arbitrary quadratic fields to generalize the Miller-Rabin primality test and enable a deterministic test for $N = c_D k p^{\ell} - 1$.
method: Algebraic framework over arbitrary quadratic fields $L = \mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{D})$, modular exponentiation, generalization of Miller-Rabin, and derivation of a Korselt-type criterion
dataset_or_scenario: Numbers of the form $N = c_D k p^{\ell} - 1$ in associated quadratic fields
results_summary: Achieves computational complexity of $\tilde{\mathcal{O}}(\log^2 N)$, requires only a single modular exponentiation, and establishes primality within milliseconds

title: Missingness-Adaptive Factor Identification in High-Dimensional Data
problem: Determining the number of factors in high-dimensional factor models when data are incomplete
key_idea: Introduces identifiable factors—those reliably recoverable despite missing observations—and proposes the Missingness-Adaptive Thresholding Estimator (MATE) for factor number determination.
method: Missingness-Adaptive Thresholding Estimator (MATE), a thresholding-based estimator that operates without data imputation and accommodates homogeneous and heterogeneous missingness
dataset_or_scenario: High-dimensional data with incomplete observations, including settings with high missingness rates and weak factor signals
results_summary: MATE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in extensive simulations and real-world applications

title: Tensor Processing with Homodyne Photonic Integrated Circuits exceeds 1,000 TOPS
problem: High-performance computing for AI demands high throughput and energy efficiency, but electronic hardware faces bottlenecks in speed and power consumption.
key_idea: A coherent homodyne photonic integrated circuit enables general matrix multiplication with >1,000 TOPS throughput via massive optical parallelism and time multiplexing.
method: Coherent homodyne integrated circuit using wafer-scale fabricated 64 thin-film lithium niobate transmitters coupled to Si/SiN computing circuits, employing time multiplexing to reduce modulator count and enable dense integration of 256×256 homodyne units.
dataset_or_scenario: Qwen2.5-0.5 billion parameter models for token generation benchmarking
results_summary: Achieves 1,000–6,000 TOPS total throughput; up to 7-bit computational accuracy across 8×8 channels at 120 Gbaud/s; 6-bit statistical accuracy across 256×100 channels at 20–128 Gbaud/s; 330 TOPS/W efficiency; 256×256 homodyne units integrated within a single reticle.

title: Scaling of Quantum Resources for Simulating a Long-Range System
problem: Understanding how quantum resources scale with system size and interaction strength when simulating long-range interacting quantum systems.
key_idea: Using pairwise logarithmic negativity as an additional criterion—beyond energy fidelity—to reliably identify the ground state in VQE simulations of long-range Ising models.
method: Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) with three structure-aware ansatze (NN, NNN, NNNN entangling blocks) inspired by string operators in the Hamiltonian.
dataset_or_scenario: One-dimensional long-range extended Ising model.
results_summary: In the non-local regime (alpha <= 1), NNN and NNNN ansatze reduce layer scaling rate by 2.5x and 3.8x relative to NN; two-qubit gate count grows quadratically with system size in non-local regime, linearly in local regime, and phase-dependently in quasi-local regime; alpha governs minimum ansatz layers required, not proximity to critical point.

title: Faster by Design: Interactive Aerodynamics via Neural Surrogates Trained on Expert-Validated CFD
problem: High computational cost of high-fidelity CFD simulations severely limits design space exploration in race-car aerodynamic development.
key_idea: A graph-based neural operator (GIST) that encodes mesh connectivity via spectral embeddings to enable accurate, discretization-invariant, and scalable surrogate modeling for complex race-car geometries.
method: Graph-based neural operator (Gauge-Invariant Spectral Transformer, GIST) with spectral embeddings encoding mesh connectivity; trained on expert-validated high-fidelity RANS data.
dataset_or_scenario: High-fidelity RANS dataset built on a parametric LMP2-class CAD model, spanning six operating conditions (straight-line and cornering regimes), generated and validated by Dallara aerodynamics experts.
results_summary: GIST achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on both public benchmarks and the proposed race-car dataset, and enables predictive accuracy suitable for early-stage aerodynamic design and interactive design-space exploration in industrial motorsport workflows.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: A Didactic Derivation of the Complete Training Cycle
problem: Existing tutorials on Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) delegate gradient computation to automatic differentiation libraries without exposing the underlying algebra, hindering pedagogical understanding and analytical insight into the full training cycle.
key_idea: A step-by-step, self-contained, didactic derivation of the complete PINN training cycle—including forward propagation of network outputs and derivatives, composite loss evaluation, explicit backpropagation with product rule handling, and parameter updates—using concrete numerical examples.
method: Manual, explicit derivation of forward and backward passes for a 1-3-3-1 multilayer perceptron applied to a first-order initial value problem; derivation of general recursive sensitivity propagation formulas; validation via hand-calculated vs. PyTorch-computed gradients.
dataset_or_scenario: A first-order initial value problem with a known analytical solution.
results_summary: The trained PINN achieves a relative L2 error of 4.290 × 10^{-4} using only the physics-informed loss, without any data from the true solution.

title: New' examples of skew fields not finitely generated as algebras
problem: Determining when associative division algebras (skew fields) are affine — i.e., finitely generated as algebras — over their central subfields, especially in transcendental and quantum/Lie-theoretic settings.
key_idea: Characterizes affineness and nonaffineness for division algebras of fractions of iterated skew polynomial rings, Weyl algebras, and quantum affine spaces over arbitrary fields.
method: Analysis of division algebras of fractions of suitably conditioned iterated skew polynomial rings, with applications to Weyl algebras and quantum affine spaces.
dataset_or_scenario: Division algebras arising from iterated skew polynomial rings, Weyl algebras, and quantum affine spaces over arbitrary fields.
results_summary: Many transcendental division algebras are verified to be nonaffine over k; division algebras of fractions of Weyl algebras and quantum affine spaces are affine over their centers exactly when finite dimensional over their centers.

title: Warm-Start Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm for QAM MIMO Data Detection
problem: Data detection in large-scale MIMO systems with higher-order QAM is challenging due to exponential complexity of classical ML detection and nonlinear symbol-to-bit mappings from Gray coding, leading to a higher-order unconstrained binary optimization (HUBO) problem.
key_idea: A hybrid quantum-classical detection framework using a warm-start linear-ramp QAOA (WSLR-QAOA) to solve the HUBO formulation of QAM MIMO detection.
method: WSLR-QAOA combining a structured warm-start from low-rank semidefinite relaxation solved via block coordinate descent (BCD) and a linear ramp parameterization for QAOA optimization.
dataset_or_scenario: Large-scale MIMO systems with higher-order quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) and Gray-coded modulation.
results_summary: The framework outperforms classical methods in SER, converges faster than standard QAOA, achieves performance close to optimal ML detector in simulation, and on IBM quantum hardware achieves near-ML performance at low SNR and maintains competitive accuracy at higher SNR despite hardware noise degradation.
limitations: Moderate degradation due to hardware noise on actual IBM quantum hardware.

title: Multi-Scale Reversible Chaos Game Representation: A Unified Framework for Sequence Classification
problem: Biological classification with interpretability remains a challenging task.
key_idea: We introduce a novel encoding framework, Multi-Scale Reversible Chaos Game Representation (MS-RCGR), that transforms biological sequences into multi-resolution geometric representations with guaranteed reversibility.
method: MS-RCGR employs rational arithmetic and hierarchical k-mer decomposition to generate scale-invariant features; it bridges traditional machine learning, computer vision on CGR-generated images, and hybrid approaches combining protein language model embeddings with CGR features.
dataset_or_scenario: synthetic DNA and protein datasets encompassing seven distinct sequence classes
results_summary: MS-RCGR features consistently enhance classification performance across all paradigms; the hybrid approach combining pre-trained language model embeddings (ESM2, ProtT5) with MS-RCGR features achieves superior performance compared to either method alone.

title: On the Independence Number of the Prime-Coprime Graph of a Finite Group
problem: Determining the independence number of the prime-coprime graph Θ(G) of a finite group G, and characterizing when Θ(G) is a split graph
key_idea: Characterizing finite groups for which the prime-coprime graph is a split graph and establishing a general lower bound and exact values for the independence number across key group families.
method: Graph-theoretic analysis combined with group-theoretic classification and structural reasoning about orders of elements and their gcd properties
dataset_or_scenario: Finite groups, specifically cyclic, dihedral, dicyclic, and semidihedral groups
results_summary: A general lower bound for the independence number of Θ(G) is established; the independence number is explicitly computed for cyclic, dihedral, dicyclic, and semidihedral groups; all finite groups G for which Θ(G) is a split graph are characterized.

title: New bounds for the area of MOTS and generalized ultra-massive spacetimes
problem: Deriving area bounds for general closed marginally trapped surfaces (MTS) without requiring stability assumptions, and characterizing spacetimes that realize these bounds.
key_idea: Area bounds for marginally trapped surfaces are determined by a constant from the Einstein tensor on the surface and another constant governing (in)stability, with stability enlarging the bounds.
method: Theoretical analysis using geometric and gravitational constraints on marginally trapped surfaces and marginally trapped tubes, incorporating Einstein tensor components and Gaussian curvature conditions.
dataset_or_scenario: General relativistic spacetimes containing closed marginally trapped surfaces, including those with positive, zero, or negative cosmological constant Λ and varying energy-momentum content.
results_summary: Derives new area bounds for MTS dependent on Einstein tensor components and instability constants; recovers the universal bound 4π/Λ for spatially stable MTS when Λ > 0; identifies 'ultra-massive spacetimes' with no event horizon where the entire external region collapses; extends similar behavior to non-positive Λ under sufficiently strong energy-momentum conditions.

title: On the quasi-monomiality of the $α$-and $δ$-invariants
problem: Existence and nature of valuations computing the $α$- and $δ$-invariants for projective klt pairs with an ample line bundle
key_idea: Quasi-monomial valuations compute the $α(X,Δ,L)$ and $δ(X,Δ,L)$ invariants for any projective klt pair $(X,Δ)$ and ample line bundle $L$, regardless of base field countability.
method: Valuation-theoretic analysis within the framework of birational geometry and K-stability
dataset_or_scenario: Projective klt pairs $(X,Δ)$ with ample line bundle $L$ over arbitrary base fields
results_summary: Proves existence of quasi-monomial valuations computing both $α(X,Δ,L)$ and $δ(X,Δ,L)$ for all projective klt pairs and ample $L$, independent of base field countability.

title: Learning Invariant Modality Representation for Robust Multimodal Learning from a Causal Inference Perspective
problem: Current multimodal affective computing models learn spurious correlations that harm generalization under distribution shifts or noisy modalities.
key_idea: We propose a causal modality-invariant representation (CmIR) learning framework that separates each modality into 'causal invariant representation' and 'environment-specific spurious representation' from a causal inference perspective.
method: A theoretically grounded disentanglement method using invariance constraint, mutual information constraint, and reconstruction constraint to separate causal invariant and environment-specific spurious representations.
dataset_or_scenario: Multiple multimodal benchmarks
results_summary: CmIR achieves state-of-the-art performance, particularly excelling on out-of-distribution data and noisy data, confirming its robustness and generalizability.

title: Progressive Online Video Understanding with Evidence-Aligned Timing and Transparent Decisions
problem: Visual agents operating in the wild must respond to queries precisely when sufficient evidence first appears in a video stream, but conventional video LLMs lack decision transparency, evidence-aligned timing, and global causal consistency under tight computational budgets in online streaming settings.
key_idea: We propose a framework that decouples reasoning control from memory integration to enable evidence-aligned timing and transparent decisions in online video understanding.
method: The framework includes the Active Thinking Decision Maker (ATDM), a transparent reasoning controller using observable progress (ρ) and confidence (c) metrics to time responses to the first-sufficient-evidence timestamp, and the Hierarchical Progressive Semantic Integration (HPSI) module, an efficient memory system using learnable multi-level aggregation tokens propagated across clips.
dataset_or_scenario: StreamingBench and OVOBench
results_summary: Thinking-QwenVL improves accuracy from 67.63% to 71.60% on StreamingBench; achieves 71.6% on StreamingBench and 46.9% on OVOBench.

title: Random-State Generation and Preparation Complexity in Rydberg Atom Arrays
problem: Understanding how efficiently generic symmetric quantum states can be prepared in Rydberg atom arrays under hardware constraints, and characterizing the relationship between interaction strength, entanglement, and state preparation fidelity.
key_idea: In the intermediate-interaction regime of Rydberg-Ising chains, random global pulse sequences enable approach to Haar-like statistics on experimentally relevant timescales, and quantum optimal control can prepare generic symmetric states with fidelity inversely related to their entanglement entropy.
method: Numerical simulation of random global pulse sequences applied to the Rydberg-Ising Hamiltonian on periodic chains; comparison with Haar-random states within symmetry sectors; quantum optimal control for state preparation using target states sampled from a broad entanglement entropy distribution.
dataset_or_scenario: Rydberg atom arrays modeled as periodic chains under the Rydberg-Ising Hamiltonian, with varying interatomic distances (strong to weak interaction regimes) and fixed evolution times.
metrics: level-spacing statistics of reduced density matrices
results_summary: Infidelities between 10^{-5} and 3×10^{-2} achieved for 9-spin target states; fidelity decreases with increasing entanglement entropy of the target state; strong-interaction regime shows blockade-limited entanglement and non-Porter-Thomas measurement probability distribution; weaker interactions yield Haar-like statistics at long times; intermediate interactions achieve this behavior on experimentally relevant timescales.
limitations: Fidelity decreases with entanglement entropy, indicating intrinsic difficulty in preparing highly entangled states under realistic constraints; results are numerical and limited to 9 spins and specific Hamiltonian parameters.
best_fit_category: quantum control and quantum many-body simulation

title: Disorder-induced non-Gaussian states in large ensembles of cavity-coupled molecules
problem: Understanding how disorder affects vibrational dynamics and nuclear wave packet behavior in cavity-modified polaritonic chemistry under collective electronic strong coupling
key_idea: Disorder leads to non-Gaussian vibrational states on short time scales at the single-molecule level, and this effect remains robust even in larger ensembles of cavity-coupled molecules.
method: Exact matrix product state simulations; comparison with semiclassical approximations (Ehrenfest and truncated Wigner)
dataset_or_scenario: Holstein-Tavis-Cummings model with incoherent photon excitation, modeling polaritonic chemistry under collective electronic strong coupling
results_summary: The Ehrenfest approximation only well reproduces ensemble-averaged observables for very large system sizes; truncated Wigner approximation fails to capture non-Gaussian effects; non-Gaussian states persist robustly for larger molecule numbers.

title: On the Effect of Quadratic Regularization in Direct Data-Driven LQR
problem: Explainability of direct data-driven linear quadratic regulation (LQR) with quadratic regularization
key_idea: Our perspective follows the parametric effect of regularization, an analysis approach that translates regularization costs from auxiliary variables to system quantities, enabling intuitive interpretations.
method: Parametric effect analysis of regularization that translates regularization costs from auxiliary variables to system quantities, enabling elimination of auxiliary variables
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
results_summary: We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach and the identified effect of regularization via simulations.

title: Recurrence analysis of quantum many-body dynamics
problem: Complex temporal behavior of observables in out-of-equilibrium quantum many-body systems resists straightforward interpretation.
key_idea: Introduce recurrence analysis—a nonlinear time-series analysis framework established for classical dynamical systems—to investigate correlated quantum many-body dynamics.
method: Recurrence plots and recurrence quantification analysis applied to time-series data of quantum observables.
dataset_or_scenario: Quenches from the paramagnetic ground state in the one-dimensional transverse-field Ising model.
results_summary: Nearly periodic recurrence plot patterns in the deeply ferromagnetic phase transition to multiscale temporal structures at criticality; recurrence quantifiers recover the critical field strength without prior knowledge of the model.

title: Classical counterparts of shortcuts to adiabaticity in nonlinear dissipative Lagrangian systems
problem: Extending shortcuts to adiabaticity (STA) from quantum dynamics to classical nonlinear dissipative Lagrangian systems
key_idea: The same STA idea can be implemented in classical nonlinear dissipative Lagrangian systems using inverse engineering of Euler-Lagrange equations with Rayleigh dissipation.
method: Inverse engineering on the Euler-Lagrange equations with Rayleigh dissipation by prescribing endpoint-stationary trajectories; comparison with time-optimal solutions and PID tracking; introduction of a single-shot correction based on one mid-course measurement.
dataset_or_scenario: Coupled r-θ manipulator
metrics: error amplification due to geometric coupling
results_summary: Geometric coupling amplifies errors and residual energy; trade-off among smoothness, speed, and robustness is highlighted; single-shot correction reduces effect of early deviations while keeping inputs nearly smooth.

title: Tell me Mr. AI, what do you see in this image?
problem: How do modern AI vision models interpret ambiguous, non-representational stimuli like Rorschach inkblots—designed to evoke subjective, affective, and symbolic human responses—compared to human perception?
key_idea: Applying the Rorschach test to AI vision models reveals systematic perceptual and semantic biases rooted in their training, exposing a fundamental divergence from human interpretive patterns.
method: Submitted the complete set of ten Rorschach inkblots to 61 ImageNet-pretrained AI models across multiple architectural families; analyzed top-ranked class predictions using psycho-semantic variables inspired by Rorschach tradition; performed statistical comparisons with human reference profiles.
dataset_or_scenario: Rorschach inkblots (ten standard images); models pretrained on ImageNet.
results_summary: All AI models showed highly non-random, systematically convergent, and inter-model-agreeing responses, but were robustly separated from human profiles—humans exhibited substantially higher affective load, semantic richness, projected agency, and variability, whereas AI models converged toward frequent, formally coherent, and perceptually stable interpretations.

title: Expected perimeter of the convex hull of planar Brownian motion stopped upon exiting the unit disk
problem: Computing the expected perimeter of the convex hull of planar Brownian motion stopped at the exit time from the unit disk
key_idea: The expected perimeter is reduced to computing the expected maximum horizontal displacement at the exit time, which is recast in terms of harmonic measure in a truncated disk.
method: Reduction to maximum horizontal displacement at exit time; reformulation using harmonic measure in a truncated disk
dataset_or_scenario: Planar Brownian motion stopped upon exiting the unit disk
results_summary: An exact expression for the expected perimeter is obtained; nontrivial bounds on the expected area are derived.
limitations: Computing the exact expected area is stated to be a much harder problem.

title: High precision micro-optical elements on fiber facets via focused-ion beam machining
problem: Scalable photon collection and beam shaping for quantum information processing using integrated micro-optical elements on optical fibers
key_idea: Single-step fabrication of micro-spherical, micro-spiral, and micro-axicon structures directly on the core of single-mode optical fibers using focused ion beam machining with nanometer-scale precision.
method: Focused ion beam (FIB) machining
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
metrics: shape accuracy of approximately λ/80 and λ/50 at λ = 780 nm
no measurable additional roughness at spatial scales relevant to visible and near-infrared operation
results_summary: Micro-concave spherical surfaces achieve shape accuracy ~λ/80 and micro-convex ~λ/50 at 780 nm; optical characterization at 633 nm confirms expected far-field donut beam patterns for micro-spiral and micro-axicon structures; Mach-Zehnder interferometry verifies azimuthal and radial phase profiles; optimized FIB process preserves optical-grade surface quality with no measurable added roughness.

title: Quantum quenches in a spin-1 chain with tunable symmetry
problem: Understanding the dynamics of interacting quantum systems far from equilibrium, particularly how symmetry tuning affects non-equilibrium behavior in spin-1 chains
key_idea: Tuning the quadrupolar interaction strength $J_q$ allows continuous interpolation between non-integrable SU(2) and integrable SU(3) symmetry regimes, revealing a new conserved quantity at the SU(3) point that governs state accessibility.
method: Time-evolving block decimation (TEBD)
dataset_or_scenario: Anisotropic spin-1 Heisenberg chain with tunable quadrupolar interaction strength $J_q$, initialized in experimentally accessible states
results_summary: The dependence of local magnetization, entanglement entropy, and spin correlations on $J_q$ is characterized; a new conserved quantity is identified at the SU(3) symmetric point, and its role in restricting the number of accessible states is theoretically explained.

title: Context-Aware Search and Retrieval Under Token Erasure
problem: Remote document retrieval reliability in RAG-like systems when query representations suffer token erasures (partial loss of query information)
key_idea: Assigning semantically adaptive redundancy to query features based on their importance improves retrieval reliability under token erasure.
method: Information-theoretic analysis; term-frequency-based query representation; TF-IDF-weighted similarity retrieval; characterization of similarity margins via multivariate Gaussian convergence; derivation of explicit error probability approximation and computable upper bounds.
dataset_or_scenario: Real-world data (used in separate data-driven evaluation with embedding-based retrieval); abstract does not specify particular dataset names or synthetic setup details.
results_summary: The vector of similarity margins converges to a multivariate Gaussian distribution, yielding an explicit approximation and computable upper bounds for retrieval error probability; numerical results support the analysis; importance-aware redundancy principles extend to modern embedding-based retrieval pipelines.

title: Security Risks of VOA-Induced Luminescence in Chip-Based quantum key distribution
problem: Implementation-level security vulnerability associated with p-n junction-based variable optical attenuators (VOAs) in integrated photonic quantum key distribution systems
key_idea: Electrically biased p-n junction VOAs emit spontaneous luminescence at 1107 nm, creating a wavelength-resolved side channel enabling wavelength-splitting attacks without disturbing quantum states.
method: Theoretical modeling and experimental demonstration using a single-photon-sensitive spectral measurement technique
dataset_or_scenario: Integrated photonic QKD transmitters containing p-n junction-based VOAs
results_summary: Emission wavelength centered at 1107 nm, spectrally separated from C-band quantum signals; even extremely weak emission leads to non-negligible information leakage.

title: Knowing When to Quit: A Principled Framework for Dynamic Abstention in LLM Reasoning
problem: LLMs waste substantial compute producing long, incorrect chain-of-thought responses; existing abstention methods lack principled guidance for dynamic mid-generation termination.
key_idea: We model dynamic mid-generation abstention as an explicit action in a regularized reinforcement learning framework with an abstention reward parameter that controls the compute-information trade-off.
method: Formal analysis and reinforcement learning framework with value-function-based abstention rule; derivation of a principled and efficient value function approximation.
dataset_or_scenario: Mathematical reasoning and toxicity avoidance tasks
results_summary: Empirical results demonstrate improved selective accuracy over existing methods.

title: Classical and quantum evolution of inflationary fluctuations
problem: Discrepancy between classical and quantum predictions for correlation functions of inflationary perturbations, especially when interactions are relevant.
key_idea: Classical and quantum correlations of inflationary perturbations diverge exponentially with the number of e-folds elapsed after a time when they are initially enforced to agree.
method: Comparison of correlation functions computed using quantum versus classical dynamics; analysis of tree-level bispectrum of primordial curvature fluctuation and one-loop power spectrum of tensor modes.
dataset_or_scenario: Inflationary cosmology scenario during and after inflation.
results_summary: The difference between classical and quantum results is exponentially sensitive to the number of e-folds elapsed from the time of agreement; classical evolution from a finite time does not produce poles in the scalar bispectrum.

title: Crossed product C*-algebras associated with non-minimal actions on the circle
problem: Understanding structural and classification properties of crossed product C*-algebras arising from non-minimal free actions of countably infinite discrete abelian groups on the circle
key_idea: Extending prior work to non-minimal free actions and obtaining a large class of unital separable nuclear, non-simple, quasidiagonal C*-algebras with stable rank one and unique tracial state
method: Analysis of crossed product C*-algebras for non-minimal free actions of countably infinite discrete abelian groups on the circle; determination of ideal structure; estimation of nuclear dimension; computation of ordered K-theory and trace pairing for G = ℤ^d
dataset_or_scenario: Non-minimal free actions of countably infinite discrete abelian groups on the circle
results_summary: Obtained a large class of unital separable nuclear and non-simple C*-algebras that are quasidiagonal, have stable rank one, and admit a unique tracial state; determined their ideal structure; established an improved uniform upper bound for their nuclear dimension; computed ordered K-theory and its trace pairing for G = ℤ^d

title: The Rise of Quantum Computing -- Take a BITE for Built Environment and Urban Microclimate Research
problem: Applying quantum computing to challenges in the built environment and urban microclimate research, such as energy management, HVAC control, electric vehicle charging network planning, renewable energy planning, and multi-objective urban design.
key_idea: Propose the 'BITE' principle (Big search, Input-light, Tiny computation, Evaluation polish) to guide researchers in selecting suitable problems for quantum acceleration in built environment and urban microclimate applications.
method: Conceptual framework development; analysis of quantum computing principles (superposition, entanglement, interference, tunneling) and their applicability to built environment and urban microclimate problems; formulation of the BITE selection heuristic.
dataset_or_scenario: Not specified
results_summary: Not specified
limitations: Current quantum hardware is still in the Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) stage, facing challenges such as noise and hardware limits.
best_fit_category: quantum computing applications in sustainable urban systems

title: Large-c BCFT Entanglement Entropy with Deformed Boundaries from Emergent JT Gravity
problem: Understanding how boundary deformations affect the von Neumann entropy of subregions in two-dimensional boundary conformal field theories (BCFTs) at zero and finite temperature
key_idea: At large central charge, the von Neumann entropy of a subregion in a BCFT with a deformed boundary is reproduced by the island entropy in an emergent setup where the BCFT acts as a bath coupled to a 2D AdS$_2$ region governed by Jackiw-Teitelboim gravity.
method: Analyzing infinitesimal global conformal boundary deformations equivalent to leading-order BCFT perturbations with the boundary displacement operator; mapping to an emergent JT gravity description via transparent boundary conditions and dilaton boundary conditions set by the BCFT deformation; relying on assumptions about BCFT spectrum and OPE coefficients.
dataset_or_scenario: Two-dimensional boundary conformal field theories (BCFTs) with deformed boundaries, at zero and finite temperature
results_summary: The von Neumann entropy in the presence of a deformed boundary is reproduced by the island entropy in the emergent JT gravity setup; the required BCFT conditions are weaker than those for holographic BCFTs, allowing for an exponentially large number of light operators.
limitations: Relies on mild assumptions about the spectrum and OPE coefficients of the BCFT; does not apply beyond the large central charge limit or infinitesimal deformations.

title: Six Llamas: Comparative Religious Ethics Through LoRA-Adapted Language Models
problem: Whether large language models fine-tuned on distinct religious corpora encode systematically different patterns of ethical reasoning
key_idea: Using LoRA-adapted variants of the same base LLM, trained exclusively on sacred and theological texts of five world religions, as instruments to probe and compare culturally embedded ethical reasoning patterns.
method: Fine-tuning six variants of Meta-Llama-3.1-8B (one unmodified control + five LoRA-adapted on Christianity, Islam, Judaism, Hinduism, or Buddhism texts); probing with 17 standardized ethical prompts; multi-temperature sampling across ten temperature settings; computing response consistency, pairwise inter-model agreement, temperature sensitivity coefficients, and run-to-run stability.
dataset_or_scenario: Sacred and theological texts of Christianity, Islam, Judaism, Hinduism, and Buddhism; 17 standardized ethical prompts spanning moral dilemmas, game-theoretic scenarios, public policy questions, and moral-psychological self-assessments.
metrics: temperature sensitivity coefficients across four prompt domains
results_summary: LoRA-adapted models produce ethical reasoning patterns systematically differentiated from the base model and consistent with their training traditions; Trolley Problem achieves 100% consistency across all models and temperatures; base model exhibits highest overall response consistency (mean 88.3%).
